title: elaeodendron xylocarpum
intro: Elaeodendron xylocarpum, also known as the stinking wood tree or black wood, is a plant species native to tropical Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Elaeodendron xylocarpum include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Elaeodendron xylocarpum may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and effectiveness for medicinal use.
